This agreement is between the residential rental provider

this is the date the agreement
A periodic (e.g. month by month) rental agreement will be formed at the end of the fixed term
and the renter stays in the property
Day rent is to be paid
The maximum bond is 1 months’ rent (u
the rental provider may ask the Victorian Civil and Administrative Tribunal (VCAT) to increase this limit
agent must lodge the bond with the
receiving payment. The RTBA will send the renter a receipt for the bond.
If the renter does not receive a receipt within 15 business days
(other than the renter’s own bank fees)
and must allow the renter to use
another form of electronic funds transfer
The renter is entitled to receive a receipt from the rental provider confirming payment of rent
(Rental provider to tick available methods of rent payment)
ervice of documents must be in accordance with the requirements of the
Just because someone responds to an email or other electronic communications, does not mean they have consented to the
service of notices and other documents by electronic methods
The renter and rental provider must notify the other party in writing if they no longer wish to receive notices or other documents by electronic methods.
The renter and the rental provider must immediately notify the other party in writing if their
Does the rental provider agree to the service of notices and other documents by
The rental provider must complete this section before giving the agreement to the renter.
insert email address, mobile phone number or other electronic contact details
Does the renter agree to the service of notices and other documents by
, mobile phone number or other electronic contact details

If there are more than four renters, include details on an extra page.
provided and maintained in good repair.
If there is a need for an urgent repair, the renter should notify the rental provider
or further information on seeking repairs
require the renter to arrange professional cleaning
or cleaning to a professional standard
at the end of the tenancy,
Professional cleaning or cleaning to a professional standard was carried out to the rented premises
and the renter was advised that professional cleaning or cleaning to a professional standard had been carried out to those premises
Professional cleaning or cleaning to a professional standard is required to restore the rented premises to the same condition they were
start of the tenancy, having regard to the condition report and
The renter must have all or part of the rented premises professionally cleaned or pay the cost of having all or part of the rented premises professionally cleaned, if
becomes required to restore the premises to the condition they were in
before the start of the tenancy, having regard to the condition report and taking into account fair wear and tear.
corporation rules apply to the premises?
attach a copy of the rules to this agreement.
on or before the date the
renter moves into the rented premises
condition report will be provided to the renter
he rental provider must ensure an electrical safety check is conducted every two years by a licensed
electrician of all electrical installations, appliances
must provide the renter with the date of the most recent safety check

If an electrical safety check of the rented premises has not been conducted within the last two years at the time the renter occupies the premises, the rental provider must arrange an electrical safety check as soon as practicable.
This safety-related activity only applies if the rented premises contains any appliances, fixtures or fittings which use or supply gas.
he rental provider must ensure a gas safety check is conducted every two years by a licensed
gasfitter of all gas installations and fittings in the rented premises and must provide the renter with the date of the most recent safety check
f a gas safety check has not been conducted within the last two years at the time the renter occupies the premises, the rental provider must arrange a gas safety check as soon as practicable.
The rental provider must ensure that the pool barrier is maintained in good repair
The renter must give written notice to the rental provider as soon as practicable after becoming aware that the swimming pool barrier is not in working order.
arrange for a swimming pool barrier to be
repaired or replaced as an urgent repair if they are notified by the renter that it is not in working order
The rental provider must provide the renter with a copy of the most recent
on the request of the renter.
if a relocatable swimming pool is
erected, or is intended to be erected,
written notice to the rental provider
must obtain any necessary approvals before
apply to any person erecting a relocatable swimming pool.
activity only applies if the rented premises is in a bushfire prone area and is required to have a water tank for bushfire safety.
If the rented premises is in a designated bushfire-prone area under section 192A of the
and a water tank is required for firefighting purposes
the rental provider must ensure the water tank and any connected infrastructure is maintained in good repair as required.
The water tank must be full and clean at the commencement of the agreement.
Any reference to VCAT refers to the Victorian Civil and Administrative Tribunal.
is entitled to quiet enjoyment of the premises
only enter the premises in accordance with the Act
must not use the premises for illegal purpose
must not cause a nuisance or interfere with the reasonable peace, comfort or privacy of
common areas. Common areas include hallways, driveways, gardens and stairwells
. Where damage occurs, the renter must notify the
must keep the premises reasonably clean.
must ensure that the premises comply with
vacant and reasonably clean when the renter moves in
must maintain the premises in good repair and in a fit condition for occupation
agrees to do all the safety
related maintenance and repair activities set out in Part
of the agreement and not remove, deactivate or interfere with safety devices on the premises.
make some modifications without seeking consent. These modifications are listed on the

at the end of the agreement,
must restore the premises to the condition it was in before they
wear and tear). This includes removing
, unless the parties agree they do not need to be removed.
must not unreasonably refuse consent for
(a deadlock is a deadlatch with at least one cylinder)
be secured with a functioning deadlock
meets the rental minimum standards for locks and window locks
be secured with a functioning deadlock must at least
be fitted with a locking device that
operated by a key from the
may be unlocked from the inside with or without a key.
to change a lock in the master key system.
a renter seeking to change a lock in th
must not give a key to a
. Refer to the Consumer Affairs Victoria website for the full list of urgent repairs and for more information, visit
Urgent repairs include failure or breakdown of any essential service or appliance
provided for hot water, cooking, heating or laundering
The rental provider must carry out urgent repairs after being notified.
renter may arrange for urgent repairs to be done if the
taken reasonable steps to arrange for the rental provider to immediately do the repairs and the re
provider has not carried out the repairs.
If the renter has arranged for urgent repairs, the renter may be reimbursed directly by the rental provider for the reasonable cost of repairs
apply to VCAT for an order requiring the rental provider to carry out
the renter cannot meet the cost of the repairs; or
the cost of repairs is more than $2,500; or
the rental provider refuses to pay the cost of repairs if it is carried out by the renter.
breakdown of facilities, fixtures, furniture or equipment
must carry out non-urgent repairs in reasonable time
apply to VCAT for an order requir
or sub-let the whole or any part of the premises without the written consent of the
e rental provider may give the renter notice to vacate if the renter
to assign or sub-let the premises
must not demand or receive a
reasonable expenses incurred by the assignment
notice of a proposed rent increase.
Rent cannot be increased more than once every
If the rental provider or agent does not provide a receipt for rent, the renter may request a receipt.
must not increase the rent under a fixed term agreement unless the agreement provides for an increase.
at any time, if the renter has agreed within the last 7 days
to do an inspection but not more than once every 6 months
conduct an open inspection to sell, rent or value the premises
a property that is for sale or rent
believe the renter has failed to
to do a pre-termination inspection where the renter has applied to have
the agreement terminated because of family violence or personal violence.
must seek consent from the rental provider before keeping a pet on the premises.
The rental provider must not unreasonably refuse a request to keep a pet
The rental provider must ensure that any smoke alarm installed in the rented premises is
correctly installed and in working condition; and
fitted with batteries or replacement batteries; and
tested at least once every 12 months in accordance with any instructions by the manufacturer of the smoke alarm.
If a smoke alarm installed in the rented premises does not meet the requirements set out
, the renter may issue a request to the rental provider for urgent repairs to the smoke alarm in accordance with section 72AA of the Act.
On receiving notice from the renter under
, the rental provider or the provider’s agent must immediately arrange for the repairs to be carried out.
Any testing, repair or replacement of a smoke alarm that is powered by a mains electricity supply must be undertaken by a suitably qualified person.
additional terms to this agreement. The terms listed
must not exclude, restrict or modify
of the rights and duties included in
dditional terms must also comply with the Australian Consumer Law (Victoria)
or example, they cannot be unfair terms
If you need extra space, attach a separate sheet.
This agreement is made under the
Each renter who is a party to the agreement must sign and date here.
If there are more than four renters, include details on an extra page.
enting section – Consumer Affairs Victoria website at
or call Consumer Affairs Victoria on

If you have difficulty understanding English, contact the Translating and Interpreting Service (TIS) on 131 450 (for the cost of a local call) and ask to be put through to an Information Officer at Consumer Affairs Victoria on 1300 55 81 81
إذا كان لديك صعوبة في فهم اللغة الإنكليزية، اتصل بخدمة الترجمة التحريرية والشفوية
على الرقم 450 131 (بكلفة مكالمة محلية) واطلب أن يوصلوك بموظف معلومات في دائرة شؤون المستهلك في فكتوريا على الرقم 81 81 55 1300.
，請打電話給口譯和筆譯服務處，電話
衹花費一個普通電話費
讓他們幫您接通維多利亞消費者事務處
اگر شما مشکل دانستن زبان انگلیسی دارید، با اداره خدمات ترجمانی تحریری و شفاهی
به قیمت مخابره محلی تماس بگیرید و بخواهید که شما را به کارمند معلومات دفتر امور مهاجرین ویکتوریا به شماره
